verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a serine protease of the kallikrein family, primarily expressed in prostate tissue. While the NCBI summary emphasizes intracellular protease function (pro-PSA cleavage), kallikreins are secreted proteases with documented extracellular activity in tissue fluid and blood. KLK2 is secreted into the extracellular compartment where it processes extracellular substrates including pro-PSA and is detected in serum / urine in both normal and pathological contexts. Expression is heavily enriched in prostate and upregulated in prostate cancer, qualifying as tissue-restricted surface / secreted biology. The protein reaches the extracellular space via constitutive secretion and regulated release; it is not a canonical surface receptor (no TM domain) but is a secreted protease with documented extracellular substrate engagement.
